items.title: Police release video of killer Bradley Murdoch filmed days before his death

children.content: In never-before-seen footage of police questioning Bradley John Murdoch in his final days, the notorious murderer repeatedly tells officers he "knows nothing" about the location of Peter Falconio's remains.

image.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

gallery.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

thumbnail.altText: Body-worn vision from a police officer shows a tall, older man speaking to two people and pointing his finger.

items.title: Three corrections officers stood down as review launched into prisoner escape

children.content: The Northern Territory Corrections Department has stood down three of its workers with pay over a prisoner escape on Sunday that triggered a more than 48-hour manhunt.

image.altText: A close up of a prison guard's badge

items.title: Man, 34, charged with murder over death of girl, 17

children.content: Days after a 17-year-old girl was found dead inside a home in the remote community of Galiwin'ku, police have charged a 34-year-old man following an autopsy.

image.altText: An aerial view of the township of Galiwin'ku on Elcho Island.

items.title: Children's commissioner quits as CLP set to pass controversial welfare changes

children.content: Shahleena Musk's resignation comes as the Northern Territory government-dominated scrutiny committee backs the Country Liberal Party's changes to child protection laws, despite widespread opposition and confusion about a key aspect of the legislation.

gallery.altText: A woman wearing a black dress looking directly at the camera.

thumbnail.altText: A woman wearing a black dress looking directly at the camera.

tags.title: Early Childhood Education and Care Services

items.title: Dozens of Aboriginal language custodians call for further revival efforts

children.content: Aboriginal language custodians release a road map for the survival and revival of Northern Territory Indigenous languages.

image.altText: An over-the-shoulder shot of an Aboriginal child, blurred back of head in view, focus on hands holding a book.

items.title: Alleged Mongols associate arrested in rural Darwin after days-long manhunt

children.content: Northern Territory police officers have arrested Isaac Thompson, 26, in Darwin River, more than 48 hours after he escaped custody during a medical transfer.

image.altText: White man with dark brown hair, brown eyes, thick brown eyebrows with a moustache and beard.

items.title: Cultural hub in Tennant Creek celebrates Warumungu culture for NAIDOC

children.content: Hundreds of people walked through the streets of Tennant Creek today as part of a NAIDOC march, finishing at the official reopening of the town's major First Nations cultural hub.

items.title: NT minister claiming travel allowance when staying at property she owns

children.content: It comes after Jo Hersey's CLP colleague criticised Labor politician Manuel Brown for claiming the same allowance at times when staying at his Palmerston home.

image.altText: A female politician sitting down and smiling, inside a parliamentary chamber.

items.title: Single mother of six hit with $6k power bill after months of no communication

children.content: A single mother of six young children is paying off a $6,000 power bill after the Northern Territory's main energy retailer failed to send her a bill for almost 12 months. She's among thousands of Jacana Energy customers facing significant bills.

image.altText: A woman with straight brown shoulder length hair in a black tshirt, sitting at her kitchen table reading her bills.

items.title: Native title holders celebrate return of 'special country' in access win

children.content: Traditional owners from the Utopia region north of Alice Springs celebrate the recognition of their native title over an area encompassing more than 4,000 square kilometres.

children.content: Collingwood wins a thriller against North Melbourne after a last ditch kick from the Roos hits the turf.

items.title: 'Not gonna talk about that': Luai dodges press conference after another Tigers loss

children.content: The Tigers lose 32-6 to the Warriors at Campbelltown Stadium, with Jarome Luai skipping the post-match press conference after a week of reports about his future with the team.

image.altText: Jarome Luai runs with the ball during a Wests Tigers NRL game.

items.title: China achieves first-ever controlled rocket recovery

children.content: China has successfully tested an experimental rocket retrieval system, state media reported, in the hope of breaking US dominance in reusable rockets.

gallery.altText: Long March-10B rocket airborne above an offshore recovery platform.

items.title: Australia's huntsman fastest spider on record, new study suggests

children.content: A species of huntsman spider found in Queensland is the fastest mover out of more than 250 spider species analysed in a new study.

image.altText: A brown spider balancing on three green twigs framed like a window at night.

items.title: Not only is the Iran war not over, it is at a dangerous inflection point

children.content: Not only is the war not over, it is once again at a dangerous inflection point, as the US and Iran revert to military options to deal with differences over their interim peace agreement.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaks in front of a US flag.

avatar.alt: A man in a suit stands in a corridor of parliament house.

items.title: Australia's most played song on radio this week draws AI criticisms

children.content: A cover of the Madonna hit Like a Prayer is at the top of the charts but criticisms have been levied at the artist behind the cover, Josh Fawaz, over his suspected use of artificial intelligence.

children.content: Footage shows two 15-tonne concrete slabs colliding with parked cars when the incident happened north of Adelaide.

items.title: Corruption watchdog's university probe ends in tears

children.content: Former University of Wollongong governance officer Alyssa White became emotional as she explained to an ICAC inquiry why she repeatedly intervened to secure jobs for friends and former colleagues.

image.altText: Woman sitting in a chair.

items.title: This Place: Boonoo Moodla

children.content: Boonoo Moodla, Billy Lights Point, has been a place of gathering and fishing for the Barngarla people for thousands of years.
As part of NAIDOC Week the ABC is looking at Indigenous peoples' connection to country, and what makes it so special.
NAIDOC Week is marking 50 years of Deadly and runs from July 5 until July 12.

image.altText: This Place: Boonoo Moodla

items.title: The Rolling Stones prove 'This never gets old!' with new album

children.content: On album #25, Foreign Tongues, rock's elder statesmen once again dismantle concerns whether men in their eighties can still make great records.

image.altText: The Rolling Stones's Mick Jagger, Keith Richards and Ronnie Wood pose and smile together.

items.title: The lowdown on lush native lawns that only need mowing once a year

children.content: If mowing the lawn is getting you down, it might be time to ditch your exotic buffalo, couch or kikuyu grass and switch to a native alternative.

image.altText: A close up of a person in work boots walking across a lush green native lawn.

items.title: Cattle farmer plants 14,000 trees to help slow flow of flood water

children.content: Jeff Imeson has planted around 14,000 trees on his property near Nimbin in a bid to help slow the flow of future floods.

items.title: Musician loses discrimination fight against orchestra over Gaza comments

children.content: The unlawful discrimination case brought by Jayson Gillham against the Melbourne Symphony Orchestra sprung from the orchestra's decision to cancel his scheduled performance over comments he made about Gaza.

image.altText: Jayson Gillham, 39, bearded, in a suit, including black tie, walks outside a court.

items.title: Eight charged over alleged plot to kill Trump, Musk and Netanyahu at UFC fight

children.content: Eight men have been accused of planning a drone and shooting attack that aimed to kill the US president and SpaceX founder at the UFC Freedom 250 event held at the White House in June.

items.title: Big Bash to play historic season-opener in India

children.content: The historic BBL game in India, between Melbourne Renegades and Perth Scorchers, is every chance to be a one-off spectacular, with officials reluctant to commit beyond 2026.

items.title: Whether in tuxedos or workwear, France can win World Cup in any fashion

children.content: France has been peerless at this World Cup. And as their superstars continue to deliver on demand, Les Bleus are proving they can win no matter the circumstances.

items.title: Police slide into DMs of underworld group chat with warning
